strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon agent memory
Well-defined method: claim-level schema with validity intervals and truth-maintenance edges
Targeted evaluation on relevant benchmarks (LongMemEval-S, LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench)
Ablation study isolates contributions of key components
weaknesses: Results are placeholders; no actual numbers to evaluate performance
Method details (e.g., linker, retriever) are described but not validated
No comparison to other truth-maintenance or temporal KG methods
Efficiency claims are based on placeholder numbers

Include statistical significance tests or error bars for reported improvements
Add a baseline comparison to a simple temporal filtering method to isolate the contribution of the graph structure
Clarify the computational complexity of the maximum-consistent-subgraph step and justify its optimality claim
